div1.entryFree:
House, vb. 1) tr. to lodge, to quarter: for ever --d where it gets possession, Err. III, 1, 106. Satan, --d within this man, IV, 4, 57. we --d him in the abbey here, V, 188. V, 188 the ship should h. him safe, Per. II Prol. 32.
2) intr. to have a lodging, to dwell: you shall not h. with me, Rom. III, 5, 190. the cod-piece that will h. Lr. III, 2, 27. we h. i' the rock, Cymb. III, 3, 8.
